metrics.top_terms: reference, corrupted, error, cannot, text, bridge, theorem, system, uncorrupted, consequence, from, corruption, state, errorclass, lean

sections.title: T1 Spine Theorem — Reference Not Reconstructible From Corruption
sections.text:
Run context: 2026-05-18
Purpose: restate T1 as the real load-bearing theorem rather than a tautology, and show how downstream OT diagnostic claims follow as consequences.

sections.title: Core Statement
sections.text:
The trivial version is:
> Error requires a reference.
That is mostly semantic.

sections.title: The real theorem is:
sections.text: > A corrupted system cannot reconstruct its own uncorrupted reference state using only resources available within the corruption.

sections.title: Formal Shape
sections.text:
Let the state space be partitioned:
```text
State = ReferenceClass ∪ ErrorClass
ReferenceClass ∩ ErrorClass = ∅
```

sections.title: Let self-generated operations be closed over the corrupted/error class:
sections.text:
```text
SelfGenerated(Op) :=
∀ x, x ∈ ErrorClass -> Op(x) ∈ ErrorClass
```
Then no finite composition of self-generated operations produces a reference state from an error state:
```text
x ∈ ErrorClass
Ops = [Op1, Op2, ..., Opn]
∀ Op in Ops, SelfGenerated(Op)
--------------------------------
(Opn ∘ ... ∘ Op2 ∘ Op1)(x) ∈ ErrorClass
```

sections.title: Therefore:
sections.text:
```text
not ((Opn ∘ ... ∘ Op1)(x) ∈ ReferenceClass)
```

sections.title: Plain Meaning
sections.text:
You cannot build the standard from the failures.
A corrupted system can rearrange corrupted material, but it cannot recover the uncorrupted reference using only corrupted resources.
It can move furniture inside the damaged house. It cannot recover the original blueprint if the blueprint is not accessible from inside the damaged system.

sections.title: Why This Matters
sections.text: If true, this one theorem gives the direction of the whole OT diagnostic chain.

sections.title: Consequence 1 — Truth Before Error
sections.text: If the standard cannot be built from corrupted states, the standard must be prior to and independent of the corruption.

sections.title: Bridge:
sections.text: > truth before error

sections.title: Consequence 2 — Corruption Is Parasitic
sections.text: Corruption does not create the reference. It degrades, distorts, or departs from it.

sections.title: Bridge:
sections.text: > evil is derivative, not co-fundamental

sections.title: Consequence 3 — Self-Repair Impossibility
sections.text: If corrupted operations remain inside the corrupted class, then self-generated repair cannot restore the uncorrupted reference.

sections.title: Bridge:
sections.text: > sign invariance / self-repair impossibility

sections.title: Consequence 4 — External Grace Required
sections.text: If self-generated operations cannot reconstruct the reference, restoration requires an operation not generated from the corrupted class.

sections.title: Bridge:
sections.text: > external grace required

sections.title: Consequence 5 — Clean Signal Requirement
sections.text:
The external operation must carry or access the uncorrupted reference.
If the external operation is itself corrupted, it only imports more corruption.

sections.title: Bridge:
sections.text: > another broken system cannot be final savior

sections.title: Consequence 6 — Internal Validity / Incarnation Direction
sections.text: If the uncorrupted reference is to restore a corrupted system without replacing it, it must operate inside the system in a compatible form.

sections.title: Bridge:
sections.text:
> incarnation direction / mediator logic
This is not yet a theorem proving incarnation, but it narrows the required operator class.

sections.title: Consequence 7 — Death-Boundary Stress Test
sections.text:
If corruption attacks coherence, the strongest possible attack is death / total system termination.
If the reference survives that attack, the reference is verified as not finally corruptible by the system.

sections.title: Bridge:
sections.text:
> resurrection as verification signal
This belongs primarily to the NT fulfillment chain.

sections.title: Consequence 8 — Distribution / Pentecost Direction
sections.text: If the uncorrupted reference can be coupled into corrupted subsystems, restoration requires a distribution or internalization mechanism.

sections.title: Bridge:
sections.text:
> Pentecost / Spirit as distributed internal correction
This belongs primarily to the NT fulfillment chain.

sections.title: What T1 Does Not Prove
sections.text: T1 does not prove product form.

sections.title: Product form says:
sections.text:
```text
one required zero collapses integrated coherence
```

sections.title: T1 says:
sections.text:
```text
corrupted systems cannot self-reconstruct the uncorrupted reference
```
Those are related, but distinct.
T1 gives the direction of restoration.
Product form gives the severity of coherence collapse.
The chain needs both.

sections.title: Lean 4 Transfer Target
sections.text:
T1 should be formalized as a closure theorem:
```text
class ErrorClosed (Op : State -> State) : Prop :=
∀ x, Error x -> Error (Op x)
theorem finite_composition_error_closed :
∀ ops x,
Error x ->
All ErrorClosed ops ->
Error (compose ops x)
```

sections.title: Then add:
sections.text:
```text
axiom disjoint_reference_error :
∀ x, Error x -> ¬ Reference x
```

sections.title: Corollary:
sections.text:
```text
Error x ->
All ErrorClosed ops ->
¬ Reference (compose ops x)
```

sections.title: Downstream Corollaries
sections.text:
Likely corollaries or dependent modules:
- `SignInvariance.lean`
- `OpennessGrace.lean`
- `ExternalGrace.lean`
- `SolutionProfile.lean`

sections.title: Review Warnings
sections.text:
Do not overstate:
> T1 proves Christianity.
It does not.

sections.title: Say:
sections.text:
> T1 proves that, under the closure definitions, corrupted systems cannot self-generate access to the uncorrupted reference.
Then bridge carefully.

sections.title: Status
sections.text: > `SPINE THEOREM CANDIDATE / HIGH PRIORITY FOR LEAN`
